schema_version: v0 — migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: The library of node-class definitions. Each record defines a KIND of node — its invariant and its variable gate-set — that /sorted binds to when it casts an intention.
why: A pipeline is just the gate-set on a node-class, so 'what kind of work is this' must be a written-down, grep-able thing, not folklore. A class cannot exist without writing down what it is for (artifact-proliferation resistance). Discovered as tickets demand them, not pre-seeded — this store starts with exactly the classes already in use.
owner: the node_classes store owns writes; the emit chokepoint validates records against the template; a class definition is ratified by Akien (signature gate).

template.class: string — the class name (referenced by tickets' node_class field)
template.what: what this kind of node is
template.why: why this kind exists as a distinct class
template.invariant: true for EVERY class: proved + feeds-back-to-origin-on-failure (Laws 3 + 8)
template.prove_gate: the check-type that proves a node of this class (proof / derivation / signature)
template.extra_gates: any gates this class hangs between challenge-at-intent and prove-at-close
template.kicks_back_on: what a red looks like, and where it routes

notes_on_the_store: Invariant across all classes (Laws 3 + 8): every node is proved and feeds back on failure. Variable per class: which gate, which check-type.
'Verdict and seal from the same hand' is a code-proof property only — for human-proved classes the reviewers are the verdict and the notary is the seal, different hands.
